SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa.txn

Class TransactionalProxy

java.lang.Object
org.javalabs.jpa.txn.DBProxy
org.javalabs.jpa.txn.TransactionalProxy
All Implemented Interfaces:
InvocationHandler
public class TransactionalProxy extends DBProxy implements InvocationHandler
Dynamic proxy for database connection and transaction management.

A dynamic proxy class (simply referred to as a proxy class below) is a class that implements a list of interfaces specified at runtime when the class is created, with behavior as described below. A proxy interface is such an interface that is implemented by a proxy class. A proxy instance is an instance of a proxy class. Each proxy instance has an associated invocation handler object, which implements the interface InvocationHandler. A method invocation on a proxy instance through one of its proxy interfaces will be dispatched to the invoke method of the instance's invocation handler, passing the proxy instance, a Method object identifying the method that was invoked, and an array of type Object containing the arguments.

This proxy class instantiates a new proxy instance for the data access object class. It will start obtain an entity manager and depending on the method attribute in the DAO class, start a transaction.

Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda

  • Method Details

    • instance

      public static Object instance(Class clazz)

    • newInstance

      public static Object newInstance(Object obj)
      Create a new proxy instance of the class as identified by this object. Proxy goes by the interface definition. Therefore it is mandatory for the object to have implemented one or more interfaces.
      Parameters:
      obj - Object whose proxy class to be created.
      Returns:
      Object New proxy instance

    • invoke

      public Object invoke(Object proxy, Method method, Object[] args) throws Throwable
      Specified by:
      invoke in interface InvocationHandler
      Throws:
      Throwable

    • destroyPersistenceContext

      private void destroyPersistenceContext(LitePersistenceContext puContext)
      Destroy the current persistence context.
      Parameters:
      puContext -

    • isTxnRequired

      private boolean isTxnRequired(Method method)
      Check the method annotation and initiate a txn (if required). Here are the rules for starting a txn: If the method has annotation RequiresNew, a new txn will always be started. If the method has annotated Required, and no transaction is currently active, then a new txn will be created. If the methood has annotation NotSupported, the a txn will never be started. Default: true.
      Parameters:
      method -

    • currentContext

      private LitePersistenceContext currentContext()
      Return the current persistence context.
      Returns:
      LitePersistenceContext

    • setCurrent

      private void setCurrent(LitePersistenceContext puContext)
      Set the persistence context as a current transactional context.
      Parameters:
      puContext - Persistent context to set as current.

    • removeCurrent

      private void removeCurrent()
      Remove the current transactional context.
